review_text: Ordered a burrtito. The portion is good for the price. decent amount of topping choices although you have to pay more for some. I ordered steak. It was alright but kind of tough and they put long strips and it was hard to bite. Wasnt a whole lot of flavor. Overall it was ok. I think chilangos has better options and flavor but los tres has better prices. I would be willing to give them another shot.

review_text:
8/6/23 edit: This place has gotten even better as I have been here more often than I care to admit. Taste is consistent, and shredded chicken is the bomb.

Food was okay, better than Qdoba(on a bad day) but not as good as Chilangos. They use packaged tortillas instead of making them fresh like Chilangos. Price seems steep, $13 for a carne asada burrito that was smaller than a Qdoba or Chilangos burrito. Verde salsa didn't taste right.
Atmosphere seems cheap. Stools are too close together, tables are poorly shrink wrapped, only a couple bottles of hot sauce.
owner_response: Hi Jon, thank you for your feedback!

review_text: So, they show $9.99 for the base choice but then charge for most of the items you get.  Add a $3.50 drink, a $1.85 bag of chips, and a $3.99 4 oz guacamole and you end up with, I kid you not, a $26 check.  $26!!!!  No wonder there is no one here!  And when I questioned how a bowl ended up $26 she read off the items and excluded all the extra charges on the bowl.  I said that doesn't add up to $26 so she flips the screen over.  5 additional items I was being charged for.  I gave it back and said nope!   They need to make pricing clearer and not nickel and dime you into a $7+ higher cost on the bowl.  Yep, not coming back!  Don't recommend you bother.   You can get a sit-down meal with service for better food for the same price or less.
owner_response:
We are very sorry to hear about your experience with us. 
We will be speaking with management to prevent this from happening in the future. 
We hope you can give us another chance!

Thank you and have a great morning

review_text: First time I ate here didn’t know what to expect but the food was amazing, pull pork on flour shell w/corn queso cheese and mozzarella shredded cheese and sour cream

review_text: Probably will not ever go back again $23 for a very small burrito bowl a drink with chips and salsa. I know inflation is crazy right now but I could have dined at a real Mexican restaurant for less than this and actually had quality food. I know it says it in the menu that they charge extra for this and that but I didn’t see this I just seen 9 dollars for a burrito bowl i was like ok not bad but than they charge you for cheese they charge you for meat they charge you for almost everything other than lettuce and rice. This place is a knock of qdoba that will not last long I can gaurantee you they will be closed by the end of the year. I could go to other places and get extra meat extra everything and not pay $23 Added a photo to show you what was $23. Good luck staying open guys!!!! And I’m a person who had no problem spending 50-100 dollars on one meal for myself when I find dine.
owner_response:

review_text:
Staying in Jackson area for a couple weeks and needed a quick bite after the gym. Saw the sign exiting the highway and decided to give it a try over Arby’s or some other fast food that was close by on Michigan Ave. When given the opportunity I prefer to spend my money with a local establishment than something I can find at home, glad Los Tres was open.

Parking is ample in this strip mall. Restaurant was very clean(an employee was actually cleaning when I entered). Friday special is $2 tacos, and although I do like a deal, I decided to skip the shells and order a burrito bowl. They have a few choices for protein, I went with Birria which was an up-charge. The food was good, $12 out the door meat was flavorful, and very much similar to your national chain bowl. Guacamole and queso are add on charges as well. Over all I think the food was quite good and reasonably priced. I may come back next Friday and try the tacos and some of the assortment of salsas as I only went with a medium salsa on my bowl and it was good, although maybe not the flavor profile I was looking for, think it was more of a smoky chipotle style. I definitely will come back. Not sure about all the negative reviews but my experience was good.  Young man making my bowl was kind, attentive, and made me aware of any possible up-charges with menu choices.
owner_response: Thank you for your comments and review we hope to serve you again!

review_text: This place is way over priced for the portion size. The food looked like it had been sitting out all day. They don't keep the food covered up in the bar. They charged me extra for items I had added in my bowl but didn't tell me it was extra. When I paid I handed the girl my card and she took my card with the gloves still on that she used to make my food. Gross. I won't be back.
owner_response:

review_text: Lots of options to choose from. Food always tastes good. And they give you good portions.
owner_response: Thank you for your feedback, Colleen. We're glad you appreciate our variety and portion sizes. It’s great to hear you enjoy the flavors we offer. Your support means a lot to us.

review_text:
We've been here twice and most recently ordered online a ground beef burrito bowl with Mexican rice, black & pinto beans, as well as 8 other ingredients for $12 and it was absolutely massive!! Easily 2+ meals and rewarms really good. You get so many options without the price jumping, but of course there are options for additional cost.

We also got a grilled steak tortilla burrito which was huge, but seemed more expensive than it should be compared to the burrito bowls since all that is being added is a tortilla shell. You chose a protein, steak being an additional $1.75 above the base price of $9.99, and you can only add 5 other ingredients, giving you fewer options than the bowls, and then the price keeps rising.

Previously, we purchased their tacos for Taco Tuesday and they were really good.

Food tastes great, but the service and atmosphere could use some work. And depending on what you order (burrito bowl vs. tortilla burrito) it is a little pricey.

I ordered my food "curbside" giving them the description of my vehicle as requested online, and when I got there they just stood inside looking at me, and they were not busy. I had to go in and get my food. I also had to turn around when I realized they forgot my chips and salsa. The employees looked miserable both times. No greetings when walking in, no smiles, no apologies, moving slow as snails looking tired and/or bored.

I'll be back again because the food tastes great and what I personally like ordering (burrito bowl) is definitely worth the money. But, if you're more concerned with customer service and a warm welcoming experience, this is not the place to go.

Very overpriced a burrito and chips with a very small cup of cheese was $21. I didn't have any guacamole or queso because both were $2 upgrade.  The burrito was not very big and cold.
If you want good Mexican food go to the real las tres restaurants I've never been disappointed there

review_text:
Quick service style restaurant.  Good food. Perfect amount of toppings and huge burritos!  The only BAD I saw was the cashier not changing gloves after taking cash, toss immediately after any transaction. Gloves give a false “cleanliness” to customers.  I know gloves aren’t cheap but if you’re just using  them over and over, what is the point?  ….Change them often…ESP after handling cash . Make sure the customer
Sees fresh gloves.  Important.

I did see a gloved employee go from register to white board (food prep) and never tossed them.
owner_response: Thank you, Shelly, for taking the time to share your feedback. We're glad to hear that you enjoyed our food and found our service efficient. Your concerns about the use of gloves are important to us as we strive to maintain the highest level of cleanliness. We will reinforce the importance of changing gloves frequently and after handling cash with our team. Your input is valuable to us, and assists in our commitment to continuous improvement. Thanks again for bringing this to our attention.
